title: Tomato Soup with Blood Orange
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no animal-derived ingredients; olive oil, tomato, blood orange, silken tofu, almond milk, and the remaining ingredients contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the recipe is entirely plant-based, low in saturated fat, and builds structure and flavor through tomato, tofu, citrus, olive oil, simmering, and infusion rather than animal fat.
intro: Tomato soup has long been a staple of both home cooking and restaurant menus, and this version brings a subtle citrus lift and a soft floral finish. Blood orange brightens the tomato without overpowering it, while lavender cream adds a quiet note of elegance. The result is smooth, balanced, and just unexpected enough to feel special.

items.prep: plus 1 to 2 tsp more if needed

stepGroups.title: Build the Soup Base
items: Heat the olive oil in a soup pot over medium heat. Add the onion and cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until very soft and lightly golden. Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Stir in the tomato paste and crushed red pepper flakes and cook for 1 to 2 minutes, stirring, until the paste darkens slightly and smells sweet and concentrated.

stepGroups.title: Simmer the Soup
items: Add the bay leaf, passata, vegetable stock, kosher salt, and black pepper. Stir well and bring to a gentle simmer. Lower the heat and cook uncovered for 20 to 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the soup is smooth, slightly thickened, and the raw edge of the passata has cooked away. Do not boil aggressively. If the soup still tastes sharp, let it simmer another 5 minutes.

stepGroups.title: Infuse the Lavender Milk
items: While the soup simmers, place the almond milk in a very small saucepan. Heat just until hot and steaming, not boiling. Remove from the heat, add the fresh lavender sprig, cover, and steep for 5 to 8 minutes. Remove the lavender sprig, then chill the milk fully. The aroma should be delicate and clean.

stepGroups.title: Make the Lavender Tofu Cream
items: Combine the silken tofu, chilled lavender-infused almond milk, olive oil, and kosher salt in a blender. Blend until completely smooth. The texture should be loose enough to drizzle in a light, scant swirl. Add 1 to 2 teaspoons more almond milk only if needed to loosen it. Chill until ready to serve.

stepGroups.title: Blend and Brighten the Soup
items: Remove the bay leaf. Blend the soup until completely smooth with an immersion blender, or carefully transfer to a blender in batches. Return the soup to low heat. Stir in 1 tablespoon blood orange juice and taste. Add up to 1/2 tablespoon more only if needed. The blood orange should lift the soup and sharpen the finish without taking over. Adjust salt and black pepper to taste.

stepGroups.title: Serve the Soup
items: Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Spoon or drizzle a scant swirl of the lavender tofu cream over each bowl. You will likely not need all of the lavender cream; use only enough to give each bowl a light, elegant finish. Keep the garnish restrained so it reads as a finish, not a second component. Serve immediately.
